title: Fermented Juicy Saudi Arabian Beets Kebabs
description: A traditional Saudi Arabian dish featuring fermented beets, marinated in a blend of spices and grilled to perfection. This egg-free friendly recipe is a nostalgic treat for anyone looking to experience the flavors of Saudi Arabia.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the beets, water, and salt. Let it sit at room temperature for 3-5 days, or until the beets are fermented and tangy.
Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Drain the beets and rinse them with cold water. Pat them dry with paper towels.
In a large bowl, whisk together the olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, cumin, paprika, and black pepper.
Add the beets to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the beets onto the wooden skewers, leaving a small space between each beet.
Grill the kebabs for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, or until the beets are tender and lightly charred.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
Let the kebabs rest for 5 minutes before serving.
